title: Component wiki is a generated, drift-gated VIEW of the graph — never a second source of truth
context: OpenWiki (LangChain) showed demand for generated repo wikis, but its freshness model is a scheduled agent re-reading git diffs — probabilistic, silently stale between runs, and billed per-token via API keys. Hunch already had every primitive to do this deterministically: component/symbol/edge graph, topic anchors + doc-anchor drift, atomic IO, and subscription-only synthesis.
decision: `hunch wiki` renders one page per active component from PUBLIC-store graph queries only (assemblePack in src/wiki/wiki.ts). Freshness is deterministic: each page's graph inputs are content-hashed (packHash) into .hunch/wiki-manifest.json (atomic write); a mismatch is a `wiki-stale` drift finding folded into computeDrift (advisory; `hunch wiki --check` is the CI gate), healed by `hunch wiki --heal` which regenerates ONLY stale pages and removes orphans. Every cited decision with a topic is pinned via the existing `<!-- hunch:topic <topic> <dec_id> -->` marker, so supersession fires the established doc-anchor-stale drift and the pre-edit hook grounds wiki edits with zero new machinery. LLM prose (Overview section) is optional garnish via SynthProvider.draftProse — subscription CLI only, feature-detected, any failure degrades to the deterministic template; the freshness hash covers graph inputs only, never prose. Page bodies carry no timestamps so identical inputs are byte-identical (idempotent regen, no git noise).
consequences: wiki-stale stays advisory in `hunch drift` (never gates the doc≠graph exit code); `hunch wiki --check` exits non-zero for repos that want a CI freshness gate
Generated pages join the doc≠graph loop: superseding a pinned decision fires doc-anchor-stale ON the page, and heal points at `hunch wiki --heal`
Sparse graphs produce thin pages — honest by design; prose leans on code excerpts to compensate
The manifest must be committed for CI drift gating (this repo's curated .gitignore allowlists .hunch/wiki-manifest.json)
renderHunchSection gained an optional root param so every assistant grounding doc advertises the wiki once adopted
alternatives_rejected: OpenWiki-style scheduled agent that re-reads diffs and rewrites prose: probabilistic freshness, silent drift between runs, regen scope guessed by a model instead of computed from the graph
Wiki as an editable/second source of truth: two truths diverge; pages are regenerable artifacts and say so in their header
Including LLM prose in the freshness hash: nondeterministic output would make every page permanently 'stale'
Rendering from store.recs() (private overlay included): committed pages are a publicly-posted leak surface — public store only, per dec_9c4289a4bb
Direct Anthropic/OpenAI API calls for prose: violates the subscription-only synthesis invariant (con_2ce3f2a547); draftProse rides the existing key-stripped run() path
